records.ety: [OE. ermite, eremite, heremit, heremite, F. hermite, ermite, L. eremita, Gr. <?/, fr. <?/ lonely, solitary. Cf. Eremite.]

defs: A person who retires from society and lives in solitude; a recluse; an anchoret; especially, one who so lives from religious motives.
quotes: He had been Duke of Savoy, and after a very glorious reign, took on him the habit of a hermit, and retired into this solitary spot.

defs: A beadsman; one bound to pray for another.
A spiced molasses cookie, often containing chopped raisins and nuts.
